Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for emails related to Vectral Systems and Rachel Whitfield to find the board renewal decision, and search for emails from Vectral relocating employees (Priya Suresh, Camille Oduya) to discover the ESA housing crisis (all 3 Denver apartments rejected Priya's 85-lb German Shepherd Atlas) and the stipend configuration failure (Camille's $3,500 enhanced stipend showing $0 since April 14 arrival).
OE 2: Search Slack for Vectral-related discussions to find Javier Morales's message in #operations reporting that all three UrbanNest apartment options in RiNo and Highlands were rejected by landlords due to Atlas's weight and breed, and that Carmen Reyes has exhausted pet-friendly inventory in those neighborhoods. Also find any threads about expense categorizer issues affecting Vectral employees.
OE 3: Search CRM for the Vectral Systems company record and related deals to find the renewal deal (at_risk stage, with an estimated $58,400 12-month value per QuickBooks estimate EST-2026-0419 and CRM engagement notes), engagement history covering the renewal negotiation timeline (Rachel's 15% demand, Mina's 10% counter-offer with service sweeteners, board meeting April 23), and the April 2026 relocation wave details.
OE 4: Search Airtable Relocations table for Vectral Systems records to find active relocations: Priya Suresh (Portland - Denver, In Progress, ESA German Shepherd), Terrence Nakamura (Austin - San Francisco, In Progress, ADA-accessible housing + split shipment), and Camille Oduya (Boston - Seattle, In Progress, enhanced $3,500 stipend).
OE 5: Search or list Airtable Stipend Transactions for Vectral employees to check for Camille Oduya's missing stipend configuration and to verify whether the earlier ExpenseBot false rejections have been resolved. Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, and Lisa Park $130 WiFi system were all flagged as pending manual correction.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices to find INV-2026-0410 ($15,900 total for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya April 2026 relocations) and identify the $400 ADA Housing Sourcing Premium line item for Terrence Nakamura that Rachel Whitfield is disputing as implicitly waived under the renewal sweeteners.
OE 7: Search Linear for Vectral and ExpenseBot-related issues to find existing tickets tracking the expense categorizer bugs (ENG-214 Vectral policy config fix, ENG-216 full audit, ENG-211 pilot monitoring, ENG-220 duplicate detection) and identify any open items that remain unresolved.
OE 8: Search for emails from Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital to find her message about the Priya Dasgupta schedule change (move shifted from May 5 to May 19 due to family medical situation), confirmation that Samantha Obi (May 8) and Marcus Liu (May 12) are on track, and her pricing question about whether the 8% volume discount applies to this initial 3-move batch.
OE 9: Search Slack for Terraform and pricing-related discussions in #finance to find internal guidance on volume pricing tiers — specifically Alejandro Fuentes's analysis that the existing Terraform pricing model covers standard domestic moves but not harder-scope relocations, and Marcus Thorne's guardrails that no blended batch pricing should go to a client without finance approval. Also search emails for Alejandro's detailed pricing breakdown (email-terraform-pricing-003) which specifies the 11-move threshold for the 8% volume discount tier.
OE 10: Search CRM for Terraform Digital company record, deals, and engagements to find the account context including the reply-all incident (Catalina's accidental pricing leak to Rachel Nguyen), the $1.85M enterprise contract, and Mina's assignment as dedicated AM.
OE 11: Search for emails related to Canopy Health, Dr. Mehta, Patricia Langford, and Fatimah Al-Rashidi to find the executive relocation status including UrbanNest temporary housing options presented (River North $1,250/wk, Streeterville $1,350/wk, Gold Coast $1,450/wk) and any outstanding items like the chain-of-custody documentation template needed from Canopy's compliance team.
OE 12: Search Slack for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta discussions to find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's consolidated status update covering the three workstreams: temporary housing (awaiting Mehta's selection), equipment packing and chain-of-custody documentation (pending Canopy compliance template), and the household move timeline targeting May 5 on-site start.
OE 13: Search CRM for Canopy Health company record and engagements to find the executive relocation deal ($12,170 for Dr. Anand Mehta, Boston - Chicago), and Patricia Langford's role as the HR contact who bypassed the usual AM to request VIP-level attention from Mina.
OE 14: Look up email addresses for Rachel Whitfield and Rachel Nguyen using contacts_search_contacts to confirm rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com before sending response emails.
OE 15: Update the Vectral renewal deal stage (via crm_update_deal_stage) to Closed Won to reflect the board's approval of the 10% Preferred Partner renewal.
OE 16: Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) on the Vectral Systems account logging the renewal approval, the three outstanding service issues Rachel flagged (Priya housing, Camille stipend, INV-2026-0410 ADA surcharge dispute), and the 48-hour resolution deadline.
OE 17: Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com acknowledging the board's renewal approval and addressing all three issues: (1) the plan to expand Priya Suresh's housing search to Capitol Hill and Baker neighborhoods with FHA-compliant landlord outreach, (2) immediate escalation to fix Camille Oduya's stipend platform configuration to $3,500, and (3) a commitment to review the $400 ADA surcharge on INV-2026-0410 with finance.
OE 18: Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com acknowledging the Priya Dasgupta schedule change to May 19, confirming Samantha Obi and Marcus Liu are on track, and providing a pricing breakdown at the standard rate for 3 moves without offering the 8% volume discount since the batch is below the 11-move threshold, per internal finance guidance.
OE 19: Create CRM engagement notes (via crm_create_engagement) on the Terraform Digital account logging the schedule change and pricing discussion, and on the Canopy Health account summarizing the Dr. Mehta relocation status from the investigation.
OE 20: Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for unresolved client-impacting items: Priya Suresh's housing crisis (no signed Denver lease, Portland lease expires May 5), Camille Oduya's stipend configuration failure ($3,500 showing as $0), and the INV-2026-0410 ADA surcharge dispute requiring finance review.
OE 21: Post a message in #customer-engagement (via conversations_add_message to channel C002) giving David Chen a heads-up on the Vectral renewal outcome (approved at 10%, but three service fires with a 48-hour deadline), the Terraform pricing question status, and the Canopy Mehta relocation status, so he is not blindsided if any of this escalates.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
